questions.question: Which is the headquarters of the International Transport Forum (ITF)?
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet, chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, approved the signing of a contract between India and France to support International Transport Forum (ITF) activities in Indian transport sector. The International Transport Forum, an inter-governmental organisation within the OECD system, is global body mandating all modes of transport. It is headquartered in Paris, France.

questions.question: Under which act, the minutes of the Monetary Policy Committee Meeting are released?
questions.explanation: According to Section 45ZL of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, the Reserve Bank publishes the minutes of the proceedings of the meeting on the fourteenth day after every meeting of the Monetary Policy Committee. As per the minutes of the MPC meeting released recently, Governor Shaktikanta Das along with other members proposed a 50 basis points hike in repo rate to 5.40 per cent.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry awards the National Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Awards?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs instituted the annual National Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Awards to recognize companies that made positive impact on the society through their sustainable CSR initiatives. The Ministry has recently announced the National CSR Awards 2020, from a three-stage process carried out during the COVID-19 pandemic.

questions.question: Which state has proposed an idea of providing kitchen garden to tackle stunting and anaemia?
questions.explanation: Odisha’s Keonjhar has a high number of children with stunted growth and pregnant women with anaemia. After rise in stunting and anaemia in lakhs of families, the Odisha government is planning to provide kitchen garden and poultry farms so that these targeted households would have access to nutritious food.

questions.question: ‘International Day Commemorating the Victims of Acts of Violence Based on Religion or Belief’ is observed in which month?
questions.explanation: International Day Commemorating the Victims of Acts of Violence based on Religion or Belief is observed on 22 August. Freedom of religion or belief, freedom of opinion and expression, right to peaceful assembly and right to freedom of association are enshrined in articles 18, 19 and 20 of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.

questions.question: Which African country has released a commemorative postage stamp to celebrate 75 years of diplomatic relations with India?
questions.explanation: Egypt released a commemorative postage stamp to celebrate 75 years of diplomatic relations between Egypt and India. Egypt’s recognition of the independence of India on 18 August 1947 marked the establishment of ties. It led to the historic Friendship Treaty in 1955. India and Egypt have had traditionally strong bilateral relations.

questions.question: ‘Mithila Makhana’, which was awarded geographical indication (GI) tag, is cultivated in which state?
questions.explanation: The government has recently awarded geographical indication (GI) tag to ‘Mithila Makhana’, to help farmers get the maximum price for their produce. Its botanical name is ‘Euryale Ferox Salisb’ and it is a special variety of aquatic fox nut. Mithila Makhana is cultivated in the Mithila region of Bihar and some parts of Nepal. It is the fifth product from Bihar after Bhagalpur’s Jardalu Mango, Katarni Dhaan (rice), Nawada’s Maghai Paan and Muzaffarpur’s Shahi Litchi.

questions.question: Which country is the host of Global AI Summit?
questions.explanation: Saudi Arabia is set to organise the second edition of the Global AI Summit in September this year, under the theme ‘Artificial Intelligence for the Good of Humanity’. The upcoming event will be participated by experts from government agencies and major technology companies around the world. It will discuss a number of topics, with the aim of finding solutions to the current challenges and maximizing the use of the AI technologies.

questions.question: Miniopterus phillipsi, which was seen in the news, belongs to which species?
questions.explanation: A team of researchers has found a new species of long-fingered bats in India and Sri Lanka, named as ‘Miniopterus phillipsi’. This is similar to a thick-thumbed bat species found in Meghalaya. The bat was named after W W A Philips in recognition of his contributions to studies on the mammals of Sri Lanka and South Asia. The specimens for this species were collected from Idulgashinna cave in Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Who received the 2022 UNESCO Peace Prize in recognition of efforts to welcome refugees?
questions.explanation: German Chancellor Angela Merkel received the 2022 UNESCO Peace Prize ‘in recognition of her efforts to welcome refugees’. Merkel received the Félix Houphout-Boigny-UNESCO Peace Prize for her ‘courageous’ decision to welcome more than 1.2 million migrants in 2015, particularly those from Syria, Iraq, Afghanistan, and Eritrea.
